question.text: If an entity does not use hedge accounting, derivatives must be accounted at:

answer.text: Fair value with changes recognized in the statement of profit and loss

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Without hedge accounting, derivatives are measured at fair value (FVTPL — Fair Value Through Profit or Loss). All gains/losses are immediately recognized in the P&L. **The Trap:** Hedge accounting allows deferring some gains/losses to equity; without it, everything goes to P&L. **Cross-Exam Context:** Derivative accounting treatment is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Fair value changes flow directly to P&L, potentially causing volatility in reported earnings.
